steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left side of blank: an independent clause—“attempts to make use of the entire visual light spectrum.”
Right side: a participial phrase—“sporting rainbow-striped goggles”—followed by an independent clause—“the rainbow-hued swimmer splashes through a wavy rainbow of water.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A colon introduces an elaboration or illustration. Here, the colon separates the main statement from the descriptive details that explain how the painting achieves its visual effect. Options A and D misuse while, which cannot properly govern a participial phrase followed by an independent clause, and option C omits the necessary boundary punctuation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
